json.html:
#include <iostream>
#include <fstream>
#include <chrono>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <sstream>
#include <execinfo.h> // for backtrace on Linux/Mac
#include <unistd.h>

class Debug {
public:
    static std::ofstream logfile;
    static bool enabled;

    static void Init(const std::string& filename = "debug.log") {
        logfile.open(filename, std::ios::app);
        enabled = true;
        Log("=== Debug session started ===");
    }

    static void Shutdown() {
        if (logfile.is_open()) {
            Log("=== Debug session ended ===");
            logfile.close();
        }
    }

    template<typename... Args>
    static void Log(const char* file, int line, const char* func, Args&&... args) {
        if (!enabled) return;
        auto now = std::chrono::system_clock::now();
        auto t = std::chrono::system_clock::to_time_t(now);

        logfile << "[" << std::ctime(&t) << "] "
                << file << ":" << line << " << func << "() - ";
        (logfile <<... << args) << std::endl;
        logfile.flush();

        std::cout << "[" << file << ":" << line << "] ";
        (std::cout <<... << args) << std::endl;
    }

    static void PrintStackTrace() {
        void* buffer[100];
        int n = backtrace(buffer, 100);
        char** symbols = backtrace_symbols(buffer, n);

        Log(__FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, "Stack trace:");
        for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) { // skip this function
            Log(__FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, " ", symbols[i]);
        }
        free(symbols);
    }
};

std::ofstream Debug::logfile;
bool Debug::enabled = false;

// Macro magic so you get __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__ automatically
#define DBG(...) Debug::Log(__FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, __VA_ARGS__)
#define DBG_STACK() Debug::PrintStackTrace()

// RAII timer: tells you how long a scope took
class ScopedTimer {
    std::string name;
    std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::time_point start;
public:
    ScopedTimer(const std::string& n) : name(n), start(std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::now()) {
        DBG("Timer started: ", name);
    }
    ~ScopedTimer() {
        auto end = std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();
        auto ms = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(end - start).count();
        DBG("Timer ended: ", name, " took ", ms, " ms");
    }
};

// Assert with stack trace
#define DBG_ASSERT(cond, msg) \
    if (!(cond)) { \
        DBG("ASSERT FAILED: ", msg); \
        DBG_STACK(); \
        std::abort(); \
    }

// Example usage
int divide(int a, int b) {
    DBG("divide called with a=", a, " b=", b);
    DBG_ASSERT(b!= 0, "Division by zero!");
    return
Sadia
json.textAsHtml:
#include <iostream>
#include <fstream>
#include <chrono>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <sstream>
#include <execinfo.h> // for backtrace on Linux/Mac
#include <unistd.h>

class Debug {
public:
static std::ofstream logfile;
static bool enabled;

static void Init(const std::string& filename = "debug.log") {
logfile.open(filename, std::ios::app);
enabled = true;
Log("=== Debug session started ===");
}

static void Shutdown() {
if (logfile.is_open()) {
Log("=== Debug session ended ===");
logfile.close();
}
}

template<typename... Args>
static void Log(const char* file, int line, const char* func, Args&&...
args) {
if (!enabled) return;
auto now = std::chrono::system_clock::now();
auto t = std::chrono::system_clock::to_time_t(now);

logfile << "[" << std::ctime(&t) << "] "
<< file << ":" << line << " << func << "() - ";
(logfile <<... << args) << std::endl;
logfile.flush();

std::cout << "[" << file << ":" << line << "] ";
(std::cout <<... << args) << std::endl;
}

static void PrintStackTrace() {
void* buffer[100];
int n = backtrace(buffer, 100);
char** symbols = backtrace_symbols(buffer, n);

Log(__FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, "Stack trace:");
for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) { // skip this function
Log(__FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, " ", symbols[i]);
}
free(symbols);
}
};

std::ofstream Debug::logfile;
bool Debug::enabled = false;

// Macro magic so you get __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__ automatically
#define DBG(...) Debug::Log(__FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, __VA_ARGS__)
#define DBG_STACK() Debug::PrintStackTrace()

// RAII timer: tells you how long a scope took
class ScopedTimer {
std::string name;
std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::time_point start;
public:
ScopedTimer(const std::string& n) : name(n),
start(std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::now()) {
DBG("Timer started: ", name);
}
~ScopedTimer() {
auto end = std::chrono::high_resolution_clock::now();
auto ms = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(end
- start).count();
DBG("Timer ended: ", name, " took ", ms, " ms");
}
};

// Assert with stack trace
#define DBG_ASSERT(cond, msg) \
if (!(cond)) { \
DBG("ASSERT FAILED: ", msg); \
DBG_STACK(); \
std::abort(); \
}

// Example usage
int divide(int a, int b) {
DBG("divide called with a=", a, " b=", b);
DBG_ASSERT(b!= 0, "Division by zero!");
return
